description: Bun support is currently verified only by a package import smoke test, not the full test suite. Either port the Deno test suite to also run under Bun in CI, or remove Bun from the list of supported platforms in docs and README.
title: Port test suite to Bun or drop Bun from supported platforms

close_reason: Picture interface fields now readonly
description: Picture interface fields (mimeType, data, type, description) are mutable. For a type that comes out of the library, all fields should be readonly to prevent accidental mutation.
title: Make Picture interface fields readonly

description:
**Current grade: A- → Path to A+**

No branded/nominal types exist for handles, IDs, or domain values. Plain strings and numbers are used everywhere, which allows type-safe but semantically incorrect usage.

**What needs to happen:**
- Brand file handles to prevent mixing or reuse after disposal
- Brand rating values to distinguish normalized (0.0-1.0) from POPM (0-255) from star counts
- Brand property keys to distinguish camelCase API keys from UPPERCASE TagLib wire keys
- Use the project's existing Brand<T, K> pattern from CLAUDE.md

**Example API:**
```typescript
type NormalizedRating = Brand<number, 'NormalizedRating'>; // 0.0-1.0
type PopmRating = Brand<number, 'PopmRating'>;             // 0-255

// Prevents: setRating(popmValue) when normalized expected
file.setRating(toNormalized(popmValue));
```

**Impact:** Prevents a class of semantic misuse bugs at compile time. Rating confusion (normalized vs POPM vs stars) is the most practical win.
title: Add branded types for domain-specific values

close_reason: Unified dual mapping systems: replaced VORBIS_TO_CAMEL/CAMEL_TO_VORBIS with toTagLibKey/fromTagLibKey from PROPERTIES. All 181 tests pass.
description:
The codebase has two parallel mapping systems for property key translation:
1. VORBIS_TO_CAMEL/CAMEL_TO_VORBIS in wasi-adapter/constants.ts — covers totalTracks, totalDiscs, compilation, year, track
2. toTagLibKey/fromTagLibKey in constants/properties.ts — built from PROPERTIES entries

These overlap and can diverge. The fix is to add the missing fields (totalTracks, totalDiscs, compilation, year, track) to PROPERTIES so a single mapping system covers everything.

Files: src/runtime/wasi-adapter/constants.ts, src/constants/properties.ts
title: Unify dual mapping systems (VORBIS_TO_CAMEL + toTagLibKey)

description:
The Tags constant in src/constants/tags.ts has mixed value styles:
- camelCase for properties in PROPERTIES (e.g., Tags.Title = 'title', Tags.AlbumArtist = 'albumArtist')
- ALL_CAPS pass-through for properties NOT in PROPERTIES (e.g., Tags.RadioStationOwner = 'RADIOSTATIONOWNER', Tags.Producer = 'PRODUCER')

This is a DX inconsistency. Once more fields are added to PROPERTIES (from the dual mapping unification), more Tags values will become camelCase. The remaining ALL_CAPS values represent truly unmapped TagLib wire names.

The JSDoc documents this, but ideally all commonly-used tags would have camelCase values.

File: src/constants/tags.ts
title: Tags constant has mixed camelCase/ALL_CAPS values

description: File I/O utilities (file.ts, write.ts, directory-walker.ts) use extensive 'globalThis as any' runtime sniffing with triple-forked Deno/Node/Bun code paths. The __taglib_wasm_runtime_override global leaks into the namespace. Consider a proper platform abstraction layer. Low risk today but fragile across runtime updates.
title: Replace globalThis runtime sniffing with proper platform abstraction

description: src/mod.ts is marked @deprecated pointing to index.ts, but it still ships with the package. Dead entry points in a 1.0 are confusing. Remove it entirely before 1.0 since it's not in deno.json exports.
title: Remove deprecated src/mod.ts

description:
isValidTagName() in src/constants/tags.ts calls Object.values(Tags).includes() which is O(n) per call. Could be optimized with a Set for O(1) lookup.

Not on a hot path currently, so this is low priority. Only worth doing if the function starts being called frequently (e.g., in a validation loop).

File: src/constants/tags.ts
title: isValidTagName uses O(n) array scan

description: Node.js requires an unstable V8 flag (--experimental-wasm-exnref) for Wasm exception handling. A 1.0 release promises stability but depends on an experimental runtime feature that could change or be removed. Investigate: when is this flag expected to stabilize in V8/Node.js? Can we fall back to a non-exnref build? Should we gate 1.0 on this flag graduating?
title: Node.js requires --experimental-wasm-exnref flag

description: All issues to address before declaring taglib-wasm 1.0 stable. Ranked: blockers (1-3), serious concerns (4-8), minor polish (9-12).
title: 1.0 Release Readiness

close_reason: readTags now returns ExtendedTag via mapPropertiesToExtendedTag; batch operations and folder-api updated too
description: readTags() returns Tag with only 7 basic fields. readMetadata() also returns tags: Tag. No Simple API function returns ExtendedTag. Users wanting albumArtist must drop to Full API. Add mapPropertiesToExtendedTag and change readTags to return ExtendedTag (backwards compatible since ExtendedTag extends Tag).
title: Add extended tag reading to Simple API

description:
When BaseAudioFileImpl calls WasiFileHandle, property keys are translated twice:
1. BaseAudioFileImpl: camelCase → ALL_CAPS (via toTagLibKey)
2. WasiFileHandle: ALL_CAPS → camelCase → ALL_CAPS (via VORBIS_TO_CAMEL/CAMEL_TO_VORBIS)

The WasiFileHandle layer does an unnecessary roundtrip. Once the dual mapping system is unified, the WasiFileHandle's own VORBIS_TO_CAMEL mapping can be removed, eliminating the double translation.

This depends on the dual mapping unification.

Files: src/runtime/wasi-adapter/file-handle.ts, src/runtime/wasi-adapter/constants.ts
title: WASI adapter double-translation optimization

description: TagLib.version() returns a hardcoded '2.1.0' string rather than dynamically querying the linked TagLib version from the Wasm module. If TagLib is updated, this silently lies. Either query it dynamically or document it clearly as the build-time TagLib version.
title: TagLib.version() returns hardcoded string instead of querying Wasm

close_reason: Fixed in commits f87fd2f and 1786fb0. All 179 tests pass including cross-backend parity.
description: The Emscripten backend returns UNKNOWN for format, codec, and containerFormat when opening WavPack (.wv), TrueAudio (.tta), and WMA (.wma) files via buffer. The WASI backend correctly identifies these formats. Causes cross-backend parity test failures.
title: Emscripten: format detection returns UNKNOWN for WavPack, TTA, WMA

description:
normalizeTagInput() in src/utils/tag-mapping.ts uses CAMEL_TO_VORBIS to convert extended tag fields, producing ALL_CAPS keys. This is the same root cause as the dual mapping system — once PROPERTIES covers all fields, normalizeTagInput can use toTagLibKey instead of CAMEL_TO_VORBIS.

This is downstream of the dual mapping unification work.

File: src/utils/tag-mapping.ts
title: normalizeTagInput produces ALL_CAPS keys instead of camelCase

description:
**Current grade: A- → Path to A+**

Node.js v22.6.0+ is required but this isn't prominently communicated. v20 LTS users will hit confusing runtime errors. This is a DX issue, not a code issue.

**What needs to happen:**
- Add Node.js version requirement to README quick start section (not buried in platform guide)
- Add an engines field to package.json (`"node": ">=22.6.0"`)
- Consider a runtime check with a clear error message on startup if version is too old
- Document why v22.6.0 is needed (WASI support, import.meta features, etc.)

**Impact:** Prevents frustrating first-use failures for the ~40% of Node.js users still on v20 LTS. Small effort, outsized DX improvement.
title: Document Node.js v22.6.0+ minimum version requirement prominently

title: Resolve 29 skipped tests in msgpack-c-api.test.ts

description:
**Current grade: A- → Path to A+**

After calling `file.getFormat() === 'MP3'`, there's no way to narrow tag fields to MP3-specific properties with type safety. The metadata mappings exist internally (src/types/metadata-mappings.ts) but aren't surfaced to the type system.

**What needs to happen:**
- Create discriminated union types keyed on FileType (e.g., MP3Tag, FLACTag, MP4Tag)
- Expose which fields are valid per format via the type system
- Allow getFormat() result to narrow the AudioFile generic (or use type predicates)
- Surface format-specific field availability (ID3v2 frames, Vorbis comments, MP4 atoms) at the type level

**Example API:**
```typescript
const file = await taglib.open('song.mp3');
if (file.getFormat() === 'MP3') {
  // TypeScript knows MP3-specific fields are available
  file.getProperty('mpegVersion'); // typed as number
}
```

**Impact:** Biggest single gap in type safety. Would eliminate an entire class of runtime errors where users set format-incompatible fields.
title: Add format-specific type narrowing for tag fields

close_reason: applyTagUpdates now uses normalizeTagInput + setProperties, matching Simple API behavior
description: applyTagUpdates() in taglib-class.ts only maps 7 basic fields via tag().setX(). Extended fields (albumArtist, composer, replayGain, etc.) in TagInput are silently dropped. Must use setProperties(normalizeTagInput(tags)) like the Simple API does.
title: Fix applyTagUpdates to use setProperties for extended fields

description: Multiple docs/README examples reference properties and options that don't exist on the actual types: FolderScanResult.totalFound, .totalProcessed, .successful; FolderScanOptions.concurrency; taglib.isWasi/.isEmscripten. Users copying doc examples will get TypeScript errors immediately. Audit all docs against actual exported types and fix discrepancies.

SonarCloud S7741 flags these but they are correct for this file. Consider marking as false positives in SonarCloud or adding a suppression comment. Internal only — no API change.
title: Apply consistent typeof pattern in runtime detector

description: Add Go package that consumes taglib_wasi.wasm via wazero (pure-Go Wasm runtime). Architecture already supports this — pure C boundary + MessagePack serialization is language-agnostic. Estimated ~2 weeks effort. Key components: Go wrapper (~500 LOC), WASI filesystem config, idiomatic Go API (ReadTags/UpdateTags), tests. No changes needed to existing Wasm binary or C/C++ code. wazero handles WASI P1 natively, msgpack via vmihailenco/msgpack.
title: Go language bindings via wazero

description: Lyrics are declared in the TypeScript type system (ComplexPropertyValueMap has LYRICS) but taglib_shim.cpp does not encode or decode them. TagLib supports lyrics via USLT frames (ID3v2), ©lyr atoms (MP4), and LYRICS Vorbis comment. The complex properties API (complexProperties/setComplexProperties) supports LYRICS with fields: text, language, description. Needs: (1) encode_lyrics() in taglib_shim.cpp parallel to encode_pictures(), (2) apply_lyrics_from_msgpack() for write path, (3) getLyrics()/setLyrics() convenience methods in AudioFile interface, (4) tests across MP3/FLAC/OGG/M4A.
title: Wire up lyrics (USLT/©lyr) complex property in shim

description: new URL('file:///C:/foo/bar.wasm').pathname returns '/C:/foo/bar.wasm' with a leading slash on Windows. Deno/Node readFile handles this, but for maximum portability consider using import.meta.resolve() or platform checks. Low priority since WASI path goes to fetch() or fs.readFile() which handle it natively.
title: Handle url.pathname leading slash on Windows in resolveWasmPath

close_reason: WITH_ZLIB=ON in both Emscripten and WASI builds; zlib cross-compiled for WASI
description: The TagLib build has WITH_ZLIB=OFF (confirmed in CMakeCache). This means zlib-compressed ID3v2 frames silently fail to decompress. Some older MP3 files (especially ~2003-era software like iTunes 4.x, older CDex) used zlib-compressed APIC frames for album art. Enabling zlib requires either bundling zlib source or using a Wasm-compatible zlib build. Low frequency but causes silent data loss when encountered.
title: Enable WITH_ZLIB in TagLib build for compressed ID3v2 frames

close_reason: Won't fix: PropertyMap must accept arbitrary string keys by design. TagLib C++ PropertyMap is open-ended, returning custom/format-specific keys beyond our 35-key PropertyKey union. Tightening would break round-trip and custom metadata. Individual accessors already have PropertyKey type safety via overloads.
description: PropertyMap is typed as { [key: string]: string[] } which means properties()/setProperties() calls lose the PropertyKey type safety that getProperty/setProperty provide. Users can pass arbitrary strings without compile-time checking. Consider using Record<PropertyKey, string[]> or a branded type for the map keys.
title: PropertyMap keys are untyped string — loses PropertyKey safety

description: pictureToDataURL in src/web-utils/data-url.ts uses binary += String.fromCodePoint(byte) in a loop, which creates O(n²) intermediate strings. For very large album art this could be slow. Should use chunks.push() + .join('') or process in fixed-size chunks instead. Internal optimization only — no API change.
title: Optimize pictureToDataURL string concatenation for large images

close_reason: Implemented in-process WASI host with real filesystem syscalls. loadWasiHost() provides path-based and buffer-based access.
description:
Evaluate approaches to replace sidecar subprocess with in-process filesystem access.

## Approaches to evaluate

### 1. jco + WASI 0.2 Component Model (primary)
- Jco 1.0 released, production-ready (Bytecode Alliance)
- WASI 0.2 with real filesystem via preview2-shim
- Node.js lacks native WASI P2, so jco is the practical JS path
- Prototype: compile taglib_wasi.wasm as WASI component, transpile with jco
- Test filesystem access in Node.js and Deno

### 2. Wasmer @wasmer/sdk with WASIX (alternative)
- Can mount real host directories (unlike @wasmer/wasi which only has MemFS)
- Previous attempt used wrong package (@wasmer/wasi) — @wasmer/sdk is the correct one
- Note: security concerns around sandbox bypass (GHSA-55f3-3qvg-8pv5)

### 3. Custom host functions (simplest)
- Skip WASI filesystem entirely
- Expose read_file(path)/write_file(path, bytes) as Wasm imports
- Essentially an in-process version of what sidecar does
- Lowest complexity, most control, but non-standard

## Success criteria
- In-process (no subprocess)
- Real filesystem access (not MemFS)
- Works in Node.js and Deno
- Latency competitive with sidecar approach
title: Research in-process filesystem access (jco, Wasmer SDK, custom imports)

Changes: setCoverArt → applyCoverArt, getCoverArt → readCoverArt, getFormat → readFormat, getPictureMetadata → readPictureMetadata. New: updatePictures(path, pics) → void, updateCoverArt(path, data, mime) → void. Old names get @deprecated markers.

close_reason: Verified complete. Emscripten 5.0.2 pinned in CI (94b2115), Deno compat regex updated for node:module change, binary rebuilt (67bc7a1), all 135 tests pass.
description: Emscripten 5.0.2 has been released. Investigate what changed and what TagLib-Wasm needs to update to stay in sync. Checklist: (1) Review Emscripten 5.0.2 changelog for breaking changes, deprecations, and new features. (2) Check if any Wasm EH behavior changed (critical — our build relies on -fwasm-exceptions). (3) Check if WASI SDK compatibility is affected. (4) Check if any build flags or CMake toolchain changes are needed. (5) Check if emsdk version pinning needs updating in CI (GitHub Actions). (6) Check if any Embind API changes affect our bindings. (7) Test build with 5.0.2 and run full test suite. (8) Document any migration steps needed.
title: Investigate Emscripten 5.0.1 → 5.0.2 update for TagLib-Wasm

Node.js and Bun both have equivalent synchronous fs APIs (fs.openSync, fs.readSync, etc.) that could serve the same role. This would enable seek-based I/O for all server-side runtimes instead of forcing Node/Bun through the buffer-based Emscripten path.

description:
## Research Findings (2026-02-23) — Fully Reverse-Engineered

### Background
Apple Sound Check targets -16 LUFS (AES TD1008). ReplayGain 2.0 targets -18 LUFS (EBU R128). Both use ITU-R BS.1770 LUFS. The stereo normalization gain offset is a fixed +2.0 dB.

### Part 1: Stereo Normalization Gain (CONFIRMED)

Validated via `afconvert --soundcheck-generate` + `afinfo` on 5 commercial tracks:

- `SC_gain = -16 - LUFS` (exact match for attenuation)
- `SC_gain = RG_gain + 2.0` (within ±0.15 dB, limited by 0.25 dB quantization)
- Boost capped to keep true peak ≤ -1 dBTP
- Maximum boost hard cap: 20 dB

### Part 2: iTunNORM 10-Field Format (FULLY DECODED)

Complete mapping verified against `afinfo` output for 5 tracks + controlled test signals:

```
Field  Description                        afinfo field
  0    g1_L (ave perceived power coeff)   sc ave perceived power coeff (L)
  1    g1_R (ave perceived power coeff)   sc ave perceived power coeff (R)
  2    g2_L (max perceived power coeff)   sc max perceived power coeff (L)
  3    g2_R (max perceived power coeff)   sc max perceived power coeff (R)
  4    g2_time_L (max power timestamp)    sc max perceived power msec (L)
  5    g2_time_R (max power timestamp)    sc max perceived power msec (R)
  6    peak_L (max sample amplitude)      sc peak amplitude (L) — 16-bit (32768 = 0 dBFS)
  7    peak_R (max sample amplitude)      sc peak amplitude (R) — 16-bit
  8    peak_time_L (peak amplitude ms)    sc peak amplitude msec (L)
  9    peak_time_R (peak amplitude ms)    sc peak amplitude msec (R)
```

### Part 3: g1/g2 Algorithm (REVERSE-ENGINEERED)

Both g1 and g2 use the same algorithm: FFT-based weighted power with a psychoacoustic frequency weighting curve.

**Algorithm:**
1. Divide audio into non-overlapping 1024-sample blocks (23.2ms at 44.1kHz)
2. Apply Hanning window to each block
3. Compute FFT (rfft → 513 bins)
4. Apply psychoacoustic frequency weighting per bin (amplitude domain)
5. Compute weighted power: P = Σ|H[k]·X[k]|² / block_size
6. g1 = round(C_g1 × mean(P_across_all_blocks) / 32768²)
7. g2 = round(C_g2 × max(P_across_all_blocks) / 32768²)
8. g2_time = block_index_of_max × block_size / sample_rate × 1000 (ms)
9. peak = max |sample| per channel (16-bit scale)
10. peak_time = timestamp of peak sample (ms)

**Constants:** C_g1 ≈ 515.8, C_g2 ≈ 497.6 (fitted via least squares on 10 channels × 5 tracks)

**Timestamp validation:** Computed g2 timestamps match Apple's `sc max perceived power msec` exactly for 4/5 tracks (rhcp, nodoubt, oasis, benharper). One track (4nonblondes) differs — the weighting curve approximation causes a different block to "win" the max, though the g2 VALUE is still within 1.7% error.

#### Accuracy

| Track | g1 error (L,R) | g2 error (L,R) |
|-------|----------------|-----------------|
| 4 Non Blondes | 1.7%, 0.3% (g2); g1 similar | 1.7%, 0.3% |
| RHCP | 0.3%, 0.4% (g1) | 5.9%, 1.0% |
| No Doubt | 0.2%, 0.5% (g1) | 0.6%, 0.7% |
| Oasis | 0.3%, 0.9% (g1) | 1.8%, 0.4% |
| Ben Harper | 3.9%, 5.0% (g1) | 0.1%, 0.3% |

**g1: 1.5% avg error, 5.3% max error**
**g2: 1.3% avg error, 5.9% max error**

Max gain error ≈ 0.25 dB → well within human JND (0.5-1.0 dB).

#### Psychoacoustic Weighting Curve (47 data points)

Measured by generating mono sine tones at each frequency with identical peak amplitude, converting through `afconvert --soundcheck-generate`, and reading g1 values. dB values relative to 1000 Hz.

```
Freq(Hz)   dB         Freq(Hz)   dB
31         -50.00     900        -0.14
40         -21.84     1000        0.00  (reference)
50         -19.05     1120       +0.60
63         -16.80     1250       +1.10
80         -14.44     1400       +1.25
100        -13.50     1600       +2.00
125        -13.20     1800       +2.97
150        -13.03     2000       +4.30
160        -12.80     2250       +4.32
180        -10.91     2500       +6.00
200         -9.20     2800       +7.65
220         -8.03     3150       +8.30  ← peak
250         -7.40     3550       +8.33  ← plateau
280         -6.14     4000       +6.80
315         -5.10     4500       +3.48
350         -4.61     5000       +3.50
400         -3.60     5600       +1.50
450         -3.46     6300       +1.50
500         -2.70     7100       +0.20
560         -2.26     8000       -2.50
630         -1.70     9000       -2.51
710         -1.35     10000      -9.40
800         -0.70     11000      -9.34
                       12500     -30.00
                       14000     -30.43
                       16000     -50.00
```

Curve shape: inverse equal-loudness contour (ISO 226), peaking at 3.15-3.55 kHz (ear canal resonance). Does NOT match any standard weighting curve (A, B, C, K, ITU-R 468).

### Part 4: Conversion Implications

**RG → SC stereo gain:** `SC_gain = RG_gain + 2.0` — trivial, exact, no audio needed.

**SC → RG stereo gain:** `RG_gain = SC_gain - 2.0` — trivial, exact, no audio needed.

**Computing full iTunNORM from audio:** Requires FFT analysis with the weighting curve. The algorithm is now fully understood and could be implemented in TypeScript. On macOS, `afconvert --soundcheck-generate` produces bit-exact Apple values.

**Converting g1/g2 between RG and SC:** Not possible without audio — g1/g2 encode per-channel perceived power using a proprietary weighting, while RG only stores a stereo gain value.

### Limitations

1. The weighting curve is empirically measured (not exact), leading to ~1-6% g value error
2. The curve was measured via sine tones; real music may expose nonlinearities not captured
3. The gist formula (g1=1000×10^(-gain/10), g2=2500×10^(-gain/10)) is WRONG — 4-103% error vs Apple's actual values

### Tools Used

- `afconvert --soundcheck-generate` — generates Apple Sound Check metadata
- `afinfo` — reveals internal SC fields (perceived power, LUFS, gain, peak amplitude)
- `ffmpeg` with `ebur128` filter — independent LUFS verification
- numpy FFT analysis — weighting curve measurement and power computation
title: Implement ReplayGain ↔ Sound Check (iTunNORM) conversion utilities

close_reason: implemented in 1.0 cleanup
description: PropertyMap supports string[] (multi-value) but the Simple API Tag interface only exposes single string values for all fields. When a file has multiple artists or genres, readTags() silently drops all but the first value. No warning, no way to detect data loss. Consider: expose arrays in Tag, add a multiValue option, or at minimum document the limitation.
title: Simple API loses multi-value PropertyMap data silently

close_reason: All PropertyMap keys mapped to camelCase with VORBIS_TO_CAMEL auto-generation
description: taglib_shim.cpp FIELD_MAP only maps 11 keys to camelCase. TagLib's PropertyMap recognizes many more well-known keys that pass through as raw UPPERCASE strings. Keys needing camelCase mapping: SUBTITLE, ORIGINALDATE, ALBUMARTISTSORT, COMPOSERSORT, LABEL, RELEASECOUNTRY, RELEASESTATUS, RELEASETYPE, ASIN. Also missing from MusicBrainz convenience API: MUSICBRAINZ_RELEASETRACKID, MUSICBRAINZ_WORKID, MUSICBRAINZ_ALBUMARTISTID, MUSICIP_PUID. These keys ARE accessible via getProperties() as raw UPPERCASE — this is about convenience mapping only.
title: Map remaining TagLib PropertyMap keys to camelCase convenience names

close_reason: Chapters encode/decode via taglib_chapters.cpp using ID3v2 CHAP frames
description: TagLib has full ChapterFrame and TableOfContentsFrame support for ID3v2. Not exposed in taglib-wasm at all. Chapters contain start/end timestamps and can embed sub-frames (title, picture per chapter). Essential for podcasts and audiobooks. MP4 also has chapter support via the chpl/chap atoms. Needs: (1) TypeScript types for chapters, (2) C++ shim encoding/decoding, (3) convenience API, (4) tests with chapter-bearing test fixtures.
title: Add chapter metadata support (ID3v2 CHAP/CTOC)
